target.title: fix(acpx): keep leaked non-openai model out of the Codex ACP thinking slot
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Spawning the Codex ACP agent fails during setup when the fleet's default/primary model belongs to a non-OpenAI provider. `sessions_spawn(runtime="acp", agentId="codex")` resolves that default (for example `google/gemini-3.1-flash-lite`) into the Codex model slot, and the Codex ACP model parser then treats the segment after the `/` as a reasoning effort. The spawn aborts with:

```
spawn_failed: Codex ACP thinking level "gemini-3.1-flash-lite" is not supported.
Use off, minimal, low, medium, high, or xhigh.
```

An explicit `thinking=low` does not help, because the model id is mis-routed before the thinking value is applied. Reported in #95780.

## Why This Change Was Made

The root cause is in the Codex ACP model translation in `extensions/acpx/src/runtime.ts`. The original parser split the ref on `/` and unconditionally treated the second segment as a reasoning effort, so `google/gemini-3.1-flash-lite` became model `google` and effort `gemini-3.1-flash-lite`, which Codex's reasoning-effort set rejects (Codex `ReasoningEffort` is the closed set {none, minimal, low, medium, high, xhigh}, codex-rs/protocol/src/openai_models.rs:40; `-c model=` is a free string forwarded to the provider, so a non-OpenAI model id is never valid there).

This revision replaces the earlier "silently drop any non-OpenAI model" approach (flagged in review as hiding explicit user intent) with a classifier whose policy is owned by each call site. `classifyCodexAcpModelRequest` returns a closed result:

- `{ kind: "override", override }` for a usable request: `openai/<model>` (prefix stripped), a bare `<model>`, `<model>/<reasoning-effort>`, or a thinking-only override.
- `{ kind: "unsupported", thinkingOverride? }` for a ref that is not a valid Codex model and is not `openai/`-qualified (a foreign `provider/model`, or a malformed `<model>/<bad-suffix>`); it carries any valid explicit thinking forward.
- An `openai/`-qualified but malformed ref (for example `openai/foo/bar`) throws immediately, since the caller explicitly named OpenAI.

The two entry points then apply opposite policies, which distinguishes a leaked default from an explicit selection without threading new provenance through the cross-package ACP runtime contract (provenance is merged away upstream in `resolveConfiguredSubagentSpawnModelSelection`, so it is not available at the acpx boundary):

- `ensureSession` (spawn) is where the inherited fleet default arrives (issue #95780 confirms the leaked value is "not anything requested for this spawn"). An unsupported model is dropped so Codex keeps its own default, while an explicit `thinking` level is still applied.
- `setConfigOption` / `acp model` is an explicit runtime control. An unsupported model now fails closed with `ACP_INVALID_RUNTIME_OPTION` and never falls through to the delegate (which previously re-injected the bad ref and reproduced the crash on a later turn).

This addresses the three review blockers:
1. Explicit selections fail clearly instead of silently falling back: an unsupported `acp model google/...` is rejected at the runtime-control path; the spawn path still drops the ambient default that #95780 is about.
2. `setConfigOption(key="model")` no longer falls through to the delegate for an unsupported model; it fails closed, with a regression test asserting the delegate is never called.
3. A malformed ref such as `gpt-5.4/ultra` is no longer treated as a droppable non-OpenAI ref at the control path; it fails closed against the documented `<model>/<reasoning-effort>` contract.

A model dropped at spawn is also removed from the session input via `withCodexSessionModel`, so the raw id never reaches the acpx delegate where `applyRequestedModelIfAdvertised` would throw `RequestedModelUnsupportedError`.

The change is confined to the Codex ACP owner boundary in `extensions/acpx/src/runtime.ts`. The Claude ACP sibling has no reasoning-effort slot, so it cannot misroute a model id into a thinking level; this issue (#95780) is specific to Codex.

Intentional limitation: an explicit non-OpenAI model passed at `sessions_spawn` time (rather than via `acp model`) is dropped rather than erroring, because provenance is merged away before the acpx boundary and Codex is OpenAI-only, so there is no valid model to honor and the spawn still succeeds on Codex's default instead of crashing. The explicit runtime-control path does fail clearly.

## User Impact

Codex ACP spawns succeed again when the fleet default is a non-OpenAI model, falling back to Codex's own default model, and an explicit `thinking` level is honored. Setting an unsupported Codex model explicitly (`acp model google/...` or a malformed `<model>/<reasoning-effort>`) now fails clearly instead of silently falling back or re-injecting a bad ref. Explicit `openai/*` model selections and `<model>/<reasoning-effort>` overrides are unchanged.

## Evidence

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/acpx/src/runtime.test.ts`: 69 passed; the new classifier, spawn-drop, and `setConfigOption` fail-closed cases fall through to the delegate (or mis-route) on the prior silent-drop revision and pass with this revision.
- `oxlint` (type-aware, config/tsconfig/oxlint.core.json) on the changed files: clean.
- `tsgo` extensions prod (`tsconfig.extensions.json`) and extensions test (`tsconfig.extensions.test.json`) lanes: clean.
- End-to-end before/after against the real runtime: see Real behavior proof below.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: the prior revision silently dropped any non-OpenAI Codex model, so an explicit `acp model google/...` (and a malformed `gpt-5.4/ultra`) fell through `setConfigOption` to the delegate, re-injecting a model Codex cannot run; the spawn path also needs to keep dropping the leaked fleet default for #95780.
Real environment tested: the real `AcpxRuntime.ensureSession` and `AcpxRuntime.setConfigOption` class paths on the prior reviewed revision (silent-drop, 00d75f8216) and on this revision (9baa49ae96), driven with the codex agent registry. The only stub is the acpx delegate that would spawn the codex-acp child process; the model classifier, the Codex command translation, the session-input handling, and the runtime-control dispatch all stay real.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: construct the real AcpxRuntime for agent "codex" and (a) call `ensureSession` with model `google/gemini-3.1-flash-lite` (no thinking, then thinking `low`), `gpt-5.4/ultra`, and an `openai/gpt-5.5` control; (b) call `setConfigOption(key="model")` with `google/gemini-3.1-flash-lite`, `gpt-5.4/ultra`, and an `openai/gpt-5.4` control, recording what the delegate receives.
Evidence after fix:
```
# BEFORE (prior silent-drop revision 00d75f8216, the reviewed head)
SPAWN   google (leaked default, no thinking) -> OK; delegate model=undefined sessionOptions=undefined
SPAWN   google + thinking=low                -> OK; delegate model=undefined sessionOptions=undefined
SPAWN   gpt-5.4/ultra (malformed)            -> OK; delegate model=undefined sessionOptions=undefined
SPAWN   openai/gpt-5.5 (control)             -> OK; delegate model="gpt-5.5" sessionOptions={"model":"gpt-5.5"}
CONTROL model=google/gemini-3.1-flash-lite   -> OK; delegate.setConfigOption CALLED value="google/gemini-3.1-flash-lite"  [re-injected]
CONTROL model=gpt-5.4/ultra                  -> OK; delegate.setConfigOption CALLED value="gpt-5.4/ultra"                  [re-injected]
CONTROL model=openai/gpt-5.4 (control)       -> OK; delegate.setConfigOption CALLED value="gpt-5.4"

# AFTER (this revision 9baa49ae96, identical inputs)
SPAWN   google (leaked default, no thinking) -> OK; delegate model=undefined sessionOptions=undefined
SPAWN   google + thinking=low                -> OK; delegate model=undefined sessionOptions=undefined
SPAWN   gpt-5.4/ultra (malformed)            -> OK; delegate model=undefined sessionOptions=undefined
SPAWN   openai/gpt-5.5 (control)             -> OK; delegate model="gpt-5.5" sessionOptions={"model":"gpt-5.5"}
CONTROL model=google/gemini-3.1-flash-lite   -> THREW ACP_INVALID_RUNTIME_OPTION; delegate.setConfigOption calls=0
CONTROL model=gpt-5.4/ultra                  -> THREW ACP_INVALID_RUNTIME_OPTION; delegate.setConfigOption calls=0
CONTROL model=openai/gpt-5.4 (control)       -> OK; delegate.setConfigOption CALLED value="gpt-5.4"
```
Observed result after fix: the leaked non-OpenAI model id is dropped at spawn (Codex starts on its own default, explicit low thinking preserved) and an unsupported model set explicitly via `setConfigOption` is rejected with `ACP_INVALID_RUNTIME_OPTION` without ever calling the delegate; `openai/*` selections are unchanged.
What was not tested: no live codex-acp child process or live provider request; the acpx process-spawn delegate was stubbed. Full build not run.

Fixes #95780
target.author: yetval
